The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in soothing respiratory issues and promoting wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Miltassia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Miltassia is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as in topical creams and ointments, to leverage its purported benefits for respiratory health and skin care.
